title: carpinus orientalis subsp. macrocarpa
intro: Carpinus orientalis subsp. macrocarpa, also known as the Oriental hornbeam, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to the mountainous regions of the Balkan Peninsula and Asia Minor. The bark and leaves of this plant are used in traditional medicine, particularly in Turkish and Middle Eastern folk remedies, to treat various health conditions due to their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Carpinus orientalis subsp. macrocarpa are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of this plant are often infused in hot water to make a tea, and the resin is sometimes used in tinctures and ointments.
